rationale: Recent negative CEX/DEX news batch covering Grinex, Rhea Finance, Bunni, eXch, and BigONE. Grinex is modeled as inactive after a reported cyberattack and full operations suspension, not dead because a final shutdown marker is not yet confirmed. Rhea Finance is modeled as limited after an April 2026 DeFi exploit and withdrawal halt. Bunni is modeled as dead after its exploit-driven shutdown. eXch is modeled as dead / regulation because it announced a May 1, 2025 shutdown amid money-laundering allegations and reported law-enforcement pressure. BigONE is modeled as active with critical hack and temporary suspension events, because reporting says user funds would be compensated and trading/deposits were expected to resume rather than a terminal shutdown.

entities.summary: Kyrgyzstan-registered, Russia-linked centralized exchange widely described as connected to sanctioned Russian crypto infrastructure. In April 2026 it suspended operations after a cyberattack reportedly stole roughly 1 billion rubles, or about USD 13-15 million.
entities.notes: Modeled as inactive rather than dead: Reuters and security research report that Grinex suspended operations after the April 2026 attack, but there is not yet a final shutdown, liquidation, or closure marker. The Garantex-successor / sanctions-evasion context should remain in notes and evidence rather than being used as a standalone death_reason.

entities.summary: NEAR-based DeFi hub combining DEX and lending functionality, formed from the Ref Finance / Burrow Finance lineage. In April 2026 it suffered a large exploit and withdrawal halt, with reported loss estimates ranging from USD 7.6 million to USD 18.4 million depending on the source and recovery accounting.
entities.notes: Modeled as hybrid because sources describe Rhea as both a primary DEX and lending / DeFi hub for NEAR. Do not mark dead: current evidence supports exploit and withdrawal-halt events but not a terminal shutdown. Loss figures vary across early reports and later post-mortem analysis; keep amount language in event descriptions rather than hard numeric fields.

entities.summary: Uniswap v4 hook-based decentralized exchange protocol that shut down after an approximately USD 8.4 million exploit left the team unable to fund the audit, monitoring, and development costs required for a secure relaunch.
entities.notes: Clean dead-side candidate. Multiple sources describe an $8.4M exploit, paused smart contracts, and later permanent shutdown due to lack of resources for a secure relaunch. Withdrawals remaining open does not prevent classifying the exchange/protocol business as dead.

entities.summary: Privacy-focused non-KYC crypto exchange that announced it would cease operations on May 1, 2025 amid allegations that it processed funds tied to the Bybit hack and amid reported transatlantic law-enforcement pressure.
entities.notes: Death reason is regulation rather than voluntary_shutdown because the shutdown was reported in direct connection with laundering allegations, a claimed transatlantic law-enforcement operation, and possible criminal charges. Keep allegations carefully worded; sources report that eXch denied being a mixer but acknowledged processing a small portion of Bybit-hack funds.

entities.summary: Centralized exchange that suffered an approximately USD 27 million supply-chain / hot-wallet breach in July 2025. The exchange said private keys and cold storage remained safe, pledged full user compensation, and described service restoration rather than a terminal shutdown.
entities.notes: Included because HEI tracks major state-changing exchange events, not only deaths. Keep entity active unless later evidence shows unresolved service failure or closure. The 2025 breach should be modeled through hack, deposit_suspended, and trading_halted events with limited event effects.

events.title: Grinex reported a major cyberattack
events.description: Grinex said it had suffered a large cyberattack that stole assets worth roughly 1 billion rubles, with outside estimates and blockchain analysis placing the loss around USD 13-15 million.
events.notes: Use hack rather than exploit because reporting describes a cyberattack on an exchange wallet / operations environment rather than a smart-contract exploit.

events.title: Grinex withdrawals and operations were suspended
events.description: Reporting described Grinex as suspending operations after the attack, with follow-on coverage describing halted trading and blocked withdrawals during the investigation.
events.notes: This is the direct status-changing event for the inactive classification.

events.title: Grinex was sanctioned by multiple jurisdictions
events.description: Reuters reported that Grinex had been sanctioned by the United States, United Kingdom, and European Union in 2025, with U.S. authorities linking it to sanctions-evasion infrastructure involving the A7A5 ruble-backed stablecoin.
events.notes: Year-level proxy event. This provides context and is not treated as the immediate cause of the April 2026 operational halt.

events.title: Rhea Finance suffered a major exploit
events.description: Rhea Finance suffered an exploit involving fake tokens, liquidity-pool manipulation, and oracle / validation or slippage-protection weakness; early reports estimated USD 7.6 million stolen, while later analysis described USD 18.4 million before recovery or freezes.
events.notes: Loss amount is intentionally described as a range / evolving estimate because early and later sources differ.

events.title: Rhea Finance withdrawals were halted
events.description: Reporting stated that withdrawals were halted while the Rhea team worked to contain further damage after the exploit.
events.notes: Keep limited until recovery or permanent shutdown evidence appears.

events.title: Bunni paused smart contracts after exploit
events.description: Bunni paused all smart-contract functions after an exploit drained an estimated USD 8.4 million across supported networks, including Ethereum and Unichain exposure.
events.notes: This is the initial crisis event that later led to the terminal shutdown.

events.title: Bunni announced shutdown after the exploit
events.description: Bunni announced it would shut down operations because the exploit left the team without enough resources to fund a secure relaunch, including audit and monitoring costs.
events.notes: Users could still withdraw assets, but protocol/business operations were being wound down.

events.title: Bunni treated as permanently closed
events.description: Follow-up reporting described Bunni as closing permanently after the exploit, with remaining funds to be distributed and contracts open-sourced.
events.notes: HEI uses 2025-10-23 as the terminal public reporting marker.

events.title: eXch cited law-enforcement pressure and laundering allegations
events.description: eXch said it had become the target of an active transatlantic operation amid allegations that Lazarus Group used the platform to launder funds from the Bybit hack; earlier reporting said eXch denied being a mixer but acknowledged processing a small portion of funds from the incident.
events.notes: Keep this as regulatory / law-enforcement-pressure context, not a finding of guilt.

events.title: eXch announced it would cease operations
events.description: eXch announced that it would cease operations on May 1, 2025, describing a management decision to cease and retreat in the hostile legal and surveillance environment.
events.notes: Announcement date follows Cointelegraph reporting of the April 17 notice.

events.title: eXch shutdown date reached
events.description: The announced eXch shutdown date was May 1, 2025, marking the terminal date used by HEI for the exchange entity.
events.notes: Use as terminal date unless archived official material later gives a more precise execution marker.

events.title: BigONE suffered a USD 27 million breach
events.description: BigONE confirmed a roughly USD 27 million hot-wallet / supply-chain breach, while stating that private keys and cold storage remained safe and that user balances would be fully covered.
events.notes: Not a death event; track as a major negative operational event.

events.title: BigONE deposits were temporarily suspended
events.description: BigONE temporarily suspended deposits while reinforcing system security after the breach, with later updates indicating deposit and trading services were restored.
events.notes: Temporary suspension only; does not change the entity status to dead or inactive.

events.title: BigONE trading was temporarily halted
events.description: Reporting described BigONE as temporarily freezing or suspending trading functions after the USD 27 million security incident while working with SlowMist to trace stolen funds.
events.notes: Temporary halt; keep entity active unless later evidence shows unresolved service failure.

evidence.title: Russia-linked Grinex crypto exchange suspends operations after cyber attack
evidence.notes: Primary reporting source for Grinex operations suspension, stolen-assets figure, sanctions context, and A7A5 allegations.

evidence.title: Sanctioned Russian Exchange Grinex and Kyrgyzstani Exchange TokenSpot Hit in USD 15 Million Theft
evidence.notes: Blockchain intelligence source for the approximately USD 15 million theft, Grinex / Garantex-successor context, and TokenSpot tie.

evidence.title: Russia-linked crypto exchange involved in sanctions evasion suspends operations after hack
evidence.notes: Supporting source for the Telegram-channel statement, operations suspension, and reported wallet losses.

evidence.title: NEAR Protocol DeFi Hub Rhea Finance Loses $7.6 Million in Oracle Exploit
evidence.notes: Used for initial USD 7.6M exploit estimate, fake-token / oracle manipulation description, and withdrawal-halt statement.

evidence.title: Rhea Finance post-mortem puts exploit losses at $18.4 million, more than double initial estimates
evidence.notes: Used for later post-mortem loss estimate and exploit mechanics as described by the Rhea team.

evidence.title: Rhea Finance $18.4M Slippage Exploit (Explained)
evidence.notes: Security analysis source for the $18.4M figure, slippage logic description, and recovery/freeze context.

evidence.title: Bunni DEX Halts Smart Contracts After Exploit Drains $8.4M Across Chains
evidence.notes: Used for exploit date, paused smart-contract functions, and USD 8.4M loss estimate.

evidence.title: Bunni DEX shuts down after $8.4 million exploit, citing lack of funds
evidence.notes: Used for shutdown announcement, lack-of-resources rationale, and continued withdrawal availability.

evidence.title: Bunni DEX Shuts Down, Cites Recovery Costs After $8.4M Exploit
evidence.notes: Used for permanent-close wording, distribution / open-source context, and shutdown rationale.

evidence.title: Crypto exchange eXch to shut down amid money laundering allegations
evidence.notes: Primary reporting source for eXch shutdown announcement, May 1 date, laundering allegations, and claimed transatlantic operation.

evidence.title: Crypto exchange eXch denies laundering Bybit's hacked funds
evidence.notes: Context source for the prior denial and the allegation that eXch was used to launder funds from the Bybit hack.

evidence.title: Crypto exchange eXch announces closure on May 1st due to money laundering allegations
evidence.notes: Secondary source confirming the May 1 shutdown date and law-enforcement-pressure context.

evidence.title: Crypto Exchange BigONE Confirms $27M Hack, Vows Full User Compensation
evidence.notes: Used for USD 27M hack, user-compensation commitment, and SlowMist cooperation.

evidence.title: BigONE crypto exchange cleaned out for $27 million in Bitcoin, Ethereum, and more
evidence.notes: Supporting source for deposit/trading suspension, service restoration notes, and user-compensation claim.

evidence.title: BigONE Stops Trading After $27M Hack
evidence.notes: Supporting source for supply-chain-attack characterization, trading freeze, and SlowMist analysis.

manual_review_checklist: Confirm no existing Grinex, Rhea Finance, Bunni, eXch, or BigONE records before canonical apply.
Apply Grinex as inactive, not dead, unless a final closure marker is found.
Apply Rhea Finance as limited / hybrid; do not mark dead.
Apply Bunni as dead / hack; it is the cleanest dead-side candidate in this batch.
Apply eXch as dead / regulation with careful allegation wording; avoid implying a court finding.
Apply BigONE as active with critical hack and temporary suspension events, not dead or inactive.
Use event ids hei_ev_000347 through hei_ev_000360 only if available.
Use evidence ids hei_src_000751 through hei_src_000765 only if available.
